name: Zoekt
description: fast trigram code search engine (Sourcegraph); lexical retrieval at monorepo scale

developer-org.value: Sourcegraph (originally created by Han-Wen Nienhuys at Google)
developer-org.comment: Created by Han-Wen Nienhuys (based on Google's internal Code Search); the original google/zoekt repo was archived Mar 2025 and Sourcegraph now maintains the active fork.

implementation-language.comment: GitHub reports the repo as ~88% Go.

first-release.comment: Earliest public commits to the original google/zoekt date to June 2016 (a Han-Wen Nienhuys commit of 2016-06-28 is on record); exact month approximate. Sourcegraph fork maintained since ~2017/2021.

status.comment: Active development; latest commits dated July 2026 as of check on 2026-07-09.

website.comment: No dedicated product landing page; the GitHub repository serves as the home.

repository.comment: Active fork; original at github.com/google/zoekt is archived/read-only.

github-stars.comment: ~1.8k stars on sourcegraph/zoekt as displayed on 2026-07-09.

form-factor.comment: Ships as a Go library plus CLI commands (zoekt-index, zoekt) and an indexserver/webserver you self-host; it is the search backend inside Sourcegraph.

standalone-usable.comment: Can be run and queried independently via CLI, web UI, JSON/gRPC API, though most widely deployed as Sourcegraph's search backend.

bundled-host.comment: Standalone engine; also embedded as the search backend inside Sourcegraph, but usable on its own so no single bundled host.

install-mechanism.comment: Installed via `go get`/`go install` (produces binaries) or the Docker image ghcr.io/sourcegraph/zoekt.

retrieval-technique.comment: Positional trigram (n=3) indexing for substring and regexp matching; purely lexical, no embeddings.

query-interface.comment: Rich query language with boolean operators, substring/regexp matching, and a sym: atom for symbol search (backed by Universal ctags).

returns.comment: Returns matching lines/fragments within files with match locations.

structural-awareness.comment: Core matching is trigram/text-based, but it uses Universal ctags to extract symbol information as a key ranking signal and to power sym: symbol queries; not tree-sitter/LSP AST retrieval.

chunking-strategy.comment: Trigram index over file contents; code is not split into chunks.

reranking.comment: Ranks results in a single pass using signals like symbol matches; no documented second-stage cross-encoder/LLM rerank.

index-location.comment: Builds on-disk shard files on the host running the indexer; no hosted index.

incremental-updates.comment: The indexserver re-indexes only repositories that have changed rather than the whole corpus; granularity is per-repo shard (a changed repo's shard is rebuilt), not per-file.

real-time-watch.comment: The indexserver automatically syncs repositories from a code host and re-indexes them without manual action; polling-based, not an instantaneous on-save file watcher.

vector-store.comment: No embeddings; uses its own trigram shard files, not a vector store.

embedding-model.comment: n-a; lexical trigram engine with no embedding model.

mcp-support.comment: No MCP server; queried via CLI, web UI, JSON API, or gRPC.

agent-compatibility.comment: Not a native agent tool; it is a code-search backend queried via API/gRPC and reached by agents indirectly through Sourcegraph/Cody rather than integrated with named agents directly.

ide-integrations.comment: Ships a web UI (webserver at localhost:6070) and CLI/terminal search; no first-party editor extensions.

language-agnostic.comment: Trigram index works over any text/source regardless of language; ctags-based symbol ranking additionally covers many languages.

supported-languages.value: Any (text-based); symbol ranking via Universal ctags covers many programming languages
supported-languages.comment: Core search is language-agnostic text; ctags improves ranking for the languages it parses.

code-leaves-machine.comment: Self-hosted OSS; index and search run entirely on your own infrastructure, nothing is sent to a third party.

local-only-option.comment: Runs fully offline; no external API dependency.

telemetry.comment: Self-hosted OSS with no documented usage-data collection.

max-repo-scale.comment: Designed for very large corpora; powers Sourcegraph's code search across huge multi-repo/monorepo deployments. Positional trigrams need ~1.2x corpus size in RAM.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Free OSS; no paid plan for the engine itself.

maturity.comment: Stable and battle-tested as Sourcegraph's production search backend for years, but primarily an infrastructure component rather than an end-user polished product.

active-maintenance.comment: Commits dated July 2026 on main as of check on 2026-07-09; actively maintained by Sourcegraph.

community-size.comment: ~1.8k stars; a niche infrastructure component with a modest direct user base, though widely deployed indirectly via Sourcegraph.
